91748211,Cloud and Infrastructure Engineer,u&u. Recruitment Partners,https://www.seek.com.au/job/91748211?type=standard&ref=search-standalone&origin=jobCard#sol=f4a7feaebc08fd333b7999fc4d7d9089d2e5e760,1h ago,1.0,2026-04-26T22:00:00+00:00,"Parkville, Melbourne VIC (Hybrid)",Engineering - Hardware (Information & Communication Technology),Full time,,"About the Role:
u&u Recruitment Partners are delighted to partner exclusively with Murdoch Children’s Research Institute (MCRI) to recruit a number of essential positions within their Technology Office.
MCRI is investing heavily in its technology ecosystem to support increasingly data-rich research and clinical innovation. As part of this, they are seeking a Cloud and Infrastructure Engineer.

This is a project-focused role within a newly established delivery squad, driving key initiatives across cloud, infrastructure and disaster recovery. You’ll work across a hybrid environment (AWS and on-prem), playing a hands-on role in delivering scalable, resilient and modern infrastructure solutions.
About Murdoch Children’s Research Institute:

Murdoch Children’s Research Institute envisions a transformative future for child health. Their institute is a hub of impactful scientific breakthroughs, particularly in early interventions, enhanced diagnosis, prevention and potential cures. They thrive on collaborative, multidisciplinary problem-solving to address challenging queries in child health.

Committed to inclusivity and respect, MCRI fosters a positive working environment that values diverse backgrounds and experiences. As a proud member of the Australian Network on Disability and a dedicated supporter of Science Australia Gender Equity (SAGE), the Institute upholds principles of equal opportunity and diverse representation.

MCRI is committed to the safety of children and young people and takes a zero-tolerance approach to all forms of child abuse, exploitation and harm. Child safety is prioritised in decision-making at all levels of the Institute.
Key Responsibilities:
Design, build and deliver cloud and on-prem infrastructure solutions across AWS-led hybrid environments
Support major initiatives including disaster recovery uplift, infrastructure modernisation and cloud migration
Lead and contribute to Linux server transformation and containerisation (OpenShift/Kubernetes)
Implement Infrastructure as Code and CI/CD-driven deployment pipelines
Work closely with DevOps, security and engineering teams to deliver scalable, automated and resilient platforms
Contribute to ITDR planning, testing (failover/failback) and resilience strategy.
About You:
Strong experience across cloud (AWS preferred) and on-prem infrastructure environments
Deep expertise in Linux systems engineering, including migration and modernisation
Proven experience with containerisation platforms (OpenShift, Kubernetes, Docker)
Demonstrated DevOps mindset, including CI/CD, automation and infrastructure-as-code
Experience working across project-based delivery environments within complex ecosystems
Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment, taking ownership across multiple initiatives.
Working at MCRI offers a range of benefits including:
Access to salary packaging options including entertainment and novated leases
Flexible working arrangements
The opportunity to contribute to Australia’s largest child health research institute
Generous personal leave and the option to purchase additional leave
Discounted staff parking and excellent public transport access
Health and well being programs including Employee Assistance Program access
Comprehensive paid parental leave for both birth and non-birth parents.
Important Information:
As this is an exclusive recruitment partnership, all enquiries and communication regarding this position should be directed to u&u Recruitment Partners.
Successful applicants will be required to undergo:
A National Police Check
A Working With Children Check (to be obtained at the candidate’s own expense).
How to Apply:
For more information or a confidential discussion please contact Killian O’ Sullivan at u&u at killian.osullivan@uandu.com quoting reference number 44907.

At u&u Recruitment Partners, we value diversity, equity and inclusion. We welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds and people with disability.

Should you require reasonable adjustments or have a preferred method of communication throughout the recruitment process, we encourage you to make a request via adjustments@uandu.com or phone the above-mentioned u&u consultant to discuss. In response to these requests, we will collaborate closely with you to implement the appropriate adjustments.
Additionally, for a barrier-free and inclusive online experience, you can access u&u’s opportunities using accessibility software Recite Me at https://www.uandu.com/jobs.
Please submit your resume in Word format only."

91747959,Mechatronics Engineer / Automation Engineer,Australia Wide Engineering Recruitment,https://www.seek.com.au/job/91747959?type=standard&ref=search-standalone&origin=jobCard#sol=901fd5adf0f339d5da47bbfdb866746b760f36b3,2h ago,2.0,2026-04-26T21:00:00+00:00,"Warragul, West Gippsland & Latrobe Valley VIC",Other (Engineering),Full time,Salary up to $130k plus super depending on exp.,"Can you see yourself taking the next step in your professional engineering career as a Mechatronics / Automation Engineer specialising in electrical, automation and control system design for our clients global manufacturing customers? Our client has a proud history of leading innovation in this space and is looking for a like-minded professional to join their business.
Your role
The design, installation, supervision, programming, testing, commissioning and fault finding of electrical, automation and control systems
To provide technical support and solutions across our client's product range, both internally and to our client's customers
To work with our client's internal sales team and their customers to define scope of works and deliver cost-effective solutions up front for projects
About you
A relevant bachelor level engineering degree, most likely in Mechatronics Engineering
Around 5 to 10 years post graduate proven experience in design, programming and commissioning of electrical automation and control systems
PLC Programing and configuration on Beckhoff PLCs and motion systems (TwinCAT 2/3) mandatory.
Experience in the development and maintenance of control software in C# and Python
Ability to do irregular travel nationally and globally in support of commissioning at our client's customers
Well-developed problem solving, interpersonal and communication skills

This is a genuine opportunity for career development in a fast paced, flexible, autonomous environment where innovation is highly valued. So, if your values align with those of our client: Innovation and Excellence
column4: and Global Reach, Local Touch, this could be the role for you.
